name: Pakistan Nuclear Arsenal
detail: ~170 nuclear warheads (SIPRI 2024 est.); delivery systems include Shaheen-III IRBM (range 2,750 km), Ghauri MRBM, Babur cruise missiles; tactical Nasr battlefield nuclear rocket; Full Spectrum Deterrence doctrine adopted 2013

name: Pakistan Army
detail: 550,000+ active personnel; one of world's largest standing armies; 9 Corps; commands FATA/KPK counter-terrorism, Kashmir LOC, Balochistan operations; budget ~$10B (2024); COAS: General Asim Munir

name: Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI)
detail: Pakistan's premier intelligence service; reports to Prime Minister but effectively independent; key role in Afghan jihad, Kashmir policy, internal political management; widely described as a 'state within a state'

name: Shaheen-III IRBM
detail: Intermediate-range ballistic missile; range ~2,750 km; covers all of India; two-stage solid-fuel; first tested March 2015; nuclear-capable

name: Gwadar Deep-Water Port
detail: CPEC flagship; Chinese-operated (China Overseas Ports Holding); 40-year lease; Arabian Sea access; strategic chokepoint alternative to Strait of Hormuz; operational Phase-I capacity: 300,000 TEU/year

name: PAF F-16 Fighting Falcon Fleet
detail: Pakistan Air Force operates ~85 F-16s (A/B, C/D variants); sold by US; subject to end-use monitoring disputes; used in 2019 aerial engagement with India (shot down Indian MiG-21, Pakistan lost one F-16 per India's claim)

name: Line of Control Fortifications
detail: 740 km of military fortifications; ceasefire agreement 2003, frequently violated; both sides maintain division-strength forces; site of regular cross-LoC firing incidents

name: PN Agosta-90B Submarine Fleet
detail: 3 French-built Agosta-90B submarines; capable of air-independent propulsion; nuclear-capable torpedo tubes; believed to carry potential sea-based nuclear deterrent capability
